name: Transaction Due Diligence
description: Full legal due diligence for M&A, investment, or financing transactions. Covers corporate, contracts, employment, IP, litigation, regulatory, tax, real estate, and data-privacy workstreams. Produces a structured report with risk ratings and deal implications for each finding. Jurisdiction-neutral — applies the governing law of the target.
taskDescriptionTemplate:
Conduct legal due diligence on the following transaction and produce a structured due-diligence report.

Matter: {{description}}

Documents provided: {{document}}

Scope:
1. Corporate: entity structure, constitutional documents, share capital, board authority, and any corporate irregularities.
2. Contracts: key commercial agreements — obligations, termination triggers, change-of-control provisions, assignment restrictions.
3. Employment: workforce terms, senior management contracts, incentive schemes, collective agreements, and redundancy exposure.
4. Intellectual property: IP ownership, registrations, licences, and any third-party IP dependencies.
5. Litigation: current and threatened proceedings, regulatory investigations, and indemnity exposure.
6. Regulatory & compliance: licences, permits, and regulatory status; data-privacy compliance.
7. Tax: historical compliance, open assessments, transfer pricing, and any structured-tax positions.
8. Real estate: title, leases, encumbrances, and planning or zoning issues (if applicable).

For each workstream: red-flag any HIGH or MEDIUM risk findings with a deal implication (condition, price, indemnity, or walk-away). Flag any missing documents as open items.
